description: Enhanced Player with glassmorphism, expanded view, queue, waveform visualization, and dynamic backgrounds. Plus Settings panel for controlling glass intensity, blur, accent colors, animation speed, and reduced motion.
steps: Create app/components/ExpandedPlayer.tsx - a full-screen or near-full-screen player overlay with: (1) Large album artwork with dynamic color background glow behind it, (2) Track info (name, artist, album) with large typography, (3) Full playback controls (play/pause, seek bar, volume, time display), (4) A simple waveform visualization using CSS bars that animate to simulate audio levels (use requestAnimationFrame with random heights synced to playing state), (5) Queue display showing upcoming tracks (pull from AudioPlayerContext - may need to extend context), (6) Glass morphism styling throughout, (7) Smooth slide-up animation from mini player, (8) Close button to collapse back to mini player. This is a client component.

Extend app/components/AudioPlayerContext.tsx to support a queue of tracks: add queue state (ItunesTrack[]), addToQueue, removeFromQueue, playNext, clearQueue functions. Add an isExpanded state and toggleExpanded function to control showing ExpandedPlayer vs MiniPlayer.

Update app/components/MiniPlayer.tsx to: (1) Apply dynamic colors from DynamicColorProvider for progress bar and subtle artwork glow, (2) Add a click handler or expand button that triggers toggleExpanded from AudioPlayerContext, (3) Conditionally render ExpandedPlayer when isExpanded is true (or render both with the expanded one as an overlay).

Create app/components/SettingsPanel.tsx - a client component rendered as a slide-out panel or modal with glass styling. Include controls for: (1) Theme toggle (light/dark) - reuse existing ThemeToggle logic, (2) Blur intensity slider (0-50px range, stored in localStorage, applied via CSS custom property --user-blur-intensity), (3) Glass opacity slider (0.3-0.95 range, stored in localStorage, applied via --user-glass-opacity), (4) Accent color picker (preset colors + custom, stored in localStorage, applied via --dynamic-accent override), (5) Animation speed selector (slow/normal/fast/none, stored in localStorage, applied via --animation-speed multiplier), (6) Reduced motion toggle (applies a class that disables all animations), (7) Compact mode toggle (reduces padding/margins site-wide via a class on html). All settings should persist in localStorage and apply immediately.

Create app/components/SettingsContext.tsx - a client context provider that reads settings from localStorage on mount and provides them to the app. Exports useSettings hook. Updates CSS custom properties on document.documentElement when settings change.
Update app/layout.tsx to include SettingsContext provider and render a settings button (gear icon) in a fixed position or in the header that opens the SettingsPanel.

Update app/globals.css to add CSS custom properties for user-configurable settings: --user-blur-intensity (default 20px), --user-glass-opacity (default 0.7 light / 0.75 dark), --animation-speed (default 1). Update all .glass-* classes to use these variables. Add .compact-mode class that reduces padding.
Add a floating action button (FAB) in the bottom-right corner (above the mini player) with glass-fab styling that opens SettingsPanel on click.
acceptance_criteria: Running `bun run build` passes with zero errors
Running `bun run lint` passes with zero errors
ExpandedPlayer displays large artwork, track info, seek bar, volume, and waveform bars
AudioPlayerContext supports queue (addToQueue, playNext) and isExpanded state
MiniPlayer can expand into ExpandedPlayer via click/button
SettingsPanel allows adjusting blur, glass opacity, accent color, animation speed, reduced motion, compact mode
All settings persist in localStorage and apply immediately via CSS custom properties
Settings gear button is accessible in the UI
verification: Run `bun run lint` and confirm zero errors
Run `bun run build` and confirm successful build with zero TypeScript errors
